title: pedicularis verticillata subsp. tangutica
intro: Pedicularis verticillata subsp. tangutica, also known as the Tangut Pedicularis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a type of parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of surrounding plants, and is native to the mountainous regions of western China. The medicinal properties of Tangut Pedicularis include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and enhance immune function, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and capsules for medicinal use.
